description_long: This is an old, single-door refrigerator with a rectangular, upright form and a muted green exterior that shows significant wear, rust, and paint chipping. The front features a large, vertically oriented recessed handle on the left side and a patch of missing paint or residue on the upper right. The sides are plain but display more signs of age and corrosion, while the back is dominated by a series of horizontal black condenser coils and a power cord trailing from the bottom. The refrigerator stands on small feet and has a flat top surface, suitable for placing small objects. The overall appearance suggests it is a utilitarian appliance, likely found in garages, basements, or on urban streets awaiting disposal.
description: A weathered, upright green refrigerator with a recessed handle, visible condenser coils on the back, and a trailing power cord. The surface is marked by rust, scratches, and paint loss, giving it a distinctly aged look.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main door with a large recessed handle and a patch of missing paint on the upper right.
description_view_1: Left side view reveals a plain, worn surface with visible rust and the power cord emerging from the back.
description_view_2: Rear view displays horizontal black condenser coils covering most of the back panel and the full length of the power cord.
description_view_3: Right side view is similar to the left, showing a plain, corroded surface and the power cord trailing on the ground.
attribute_door_handle_style: Large, vertically recessed handle integrated into the left side of the door.
attribute_vent_or_grille_pattern: Horizontal black condenser coils covering the rear panel.
